ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'beef sandwich' class (FOODON_00002473); no specific BBQ beef brisket sandwich class exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers beef sandwich as the nearest defensible parent; no specific BBQ brisket sub code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for BBQ beef brisket sandwich found in the SNOMED CT food hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One six-inch Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket sandwich (approximately 250 g) served ala carte without a drink or side.

nutrient_source.title: Beef brisket sandwich, with condiments — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; adapted with Subway Singapore nutritional disclosure for BBQ beef brisket six-inch sub
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for 'Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Sandwich' exists. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for beef sandwich with condiments (FDC ID 172136) and cross-referenced with Subway Singapore published nutrition data for the BBQ beef brisket six-inch sub. Per-100g values are derived from a 250 g serving estimate. Subway Singapore nutrition data used as secondary corroboration only; USDA SR Legacy is the primary approved source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; mixed beef sandwich / sub roll composite
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Sandwich found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 48 is estimated from the analogous 'submarine sandwich' entry (GI ~49, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283) and wheat bread roll GI (~50, low-medium boundary). BBQ sauce adds fermentable sugars but the protein and fat content of brisket suppresses postprandial glucose response. GI 48 (low category) is a defensible conservative estimate. GL per serving = (48 × 64.0 g carbohydrate per 250 g serving) / 100 = 30.7.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~560 mg per 100 g; ~1400 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sandwich due to its very high sodium load (~1400 mg per serving), which represents approximately 61% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; request reduced-sodium condiments and avoid adding pickles or extra sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~30.7 per serving) and added sugars from BBQ sauce (~17 g per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this sandwich as the BBQ sauce contributes significant added sugars (~17 g per serving) and the overall glycemic load is moderate (~30.7); choosing a smaller portion, requesting less BBQ sauce, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef brisket (~2.4 g per 100 g; ~6 g per serving) and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this sandwich occasionally and in small portions, as the combination of saturated fat from slow-smoked beef brisket and high sodium from BBQ sauce and condiments may adversely affect blood pressure and LDL cholesterol levels.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Sandwich is a Western-style sub sandwich featuring slow-smoked beef brisket glazed with a sweet and tangy BBQ sauce, served in a soft wheat bread roll with fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and pickles. The beef brisket is characteristically tender and deeply flavoured from the smoking process, with a rich umami profile complemented by the caramelised sweetness of the BBQ sauce. In Singapore, this sandwich is available at Subway outlets and is sold ala carte, with bread type subject to availability. Nutritionally, a 250 g serving provides approximately 545 kcal, 32 g protein, 18 g fat, and 64 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is high (~1400 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The BBQ sauce contributes notable added sugars (~17 g per serving), which is relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low category), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~30.7) due to the large carbohydrate portion from the bread roll. The sandwich is a good source of protein, iron, zinc, and B vitamins from the beef brisket. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on gluten-free diets. Halal status depends on the specific restaurant and meat sourcing.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Subway BBQ beef brisket sandwich?

confidence_reason: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry for Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Sandwich exists. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest USDA SR Legacy beef sandwich composite and cross-referenced with Subway Singapore nutritional disclosures. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database submarine sandwich entry. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy beef sandwich source entry. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are estimated from USDA SR Legacy beef sandwich composite (FDC ID 172136) and are approximate. Serving size of 250 g is an estimate for a six-inch ala carte sub; actual weight varies by bread type and vegetable loading. Halal status is not confirmed; Subway Singapore outlets may serve halal-certified products but this varies by outlet. Bread options are subject to availability as noted in the source record. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database submarine sandwich entry (Atkinson et al. 2008). GL per serving calculated as (48 × 64.0) / 100 = 30.7.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from scratch. Adapted nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy beef sandwich composite. Added all mandatory multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Estimated GI from University of Sydney GI Database analogous submarine sandwich entry. Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) in notes. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 criteria passed on first iteration.
